verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the F1 beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase, a core catalytic component of the inner mitochondrial membrane complex. The NCBI summary and HGNC gene-group classification ('Mitochondrial complex V: ATP synthase subunits') establish this as a mitochondrial matrix-facing or inner-membrane-resident protein. ATP synthase F1 is a soluble catalytic core anchored to the membrane-spanning Fo component; the beta subunit itself lacks a transmembrane domain and does not reach the outer face of the plasma membrane. No aliases, CD designation, or therapeutic targeting signal indicates surface accessibility. The protein is mitochondrial-internal by both structure and function.
